intro: Sericostoma kotschyi, commonly known as the Kotschy's fumitory, is a plant species in the family Papaveraceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sericostoma kotschyi include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sericostoma kotschyi is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
